rows.proposed_default: Enable the remaining clean-pass error rules now: all currently clean E checks plus the probed call-signature and operand/runtime subset E1120,E1121,E1123,E1124,E1125,E1126,E1130,E1131,E1133,E1136.
rows.rationale: The repo already passes all E checks clean, CI already runs make pylint, and this is the lowest-noise way to expand coverage without destabilizing the existing staged rollout.
rows.text: Should the next tranche default to enabling the remaining clean-pass E rules immediately, rather than mixing in noisier warning families?

rows.proposed_default: Add the clean high-signal warning checks now: W0102,W0106,W0120,W0123,W0125,W0133,W0150,W0201,W0231,W0237.
rows.rationale: These were explicitly probed and pass clean, and they sit in the defect-prevention bucket rather than style-only lint, so they increase bug-catching value at effectively zero immediate cost.
rows.text: Which clean warning rules belong in the same enable-now tranche because they target real runtime or control-flow mistakes instead of style concerns?

rows.proposed_default: Treat W1514,W1309,W0107 as the fix-first tranche and clear the known small set before enabling them repo-wide.
rows.rationale: The known violations are few and trivial: two unspecified encodings, four static f-strings in layout.py plus one in ui.py, and three unnecessary pass statements in dataclass subclasses. This is a short cleanup pass with clear edits.
rows.text: Should the next cleanup tranche focus on the tiny obvious warning set that can likely be fixed in one pass before those rules are enabled?

rows.proposed_default: Defer W0108, defer W0621, and hold W0640 for a targeted correctness review instead of bundling them into the next broad tranche.
rows.rationale: W0108 is likely noisy, W0621 appears lower leverage, and W0640 may indicate a real closure bug but should be inspected on its own rather than turning on a family that could widen review load unexpectedly.
rows.text: Which warning candidates should be explicitly deferred because they are either noisy, require policy discussion, or deserve targeted review instead of immediate rollout?

rows.proposed_default: Use a two-step sequence: Step 1 enable the clean-pass E set plus the clean defect-oriented W subset now; Step 2 fix and then enable W1514,W1309,W0107; leave deferred items out of this cycle.
rows.rationale: This expands defect coverage immediately, keeps CI noise near zero, and gives each remaining warning family a clear rollout path under the existing make pylint and CI job.
rows.text: What is the smallest rollout sequence that preserves boring CI while still moving the repository meaningfully forward on pylint coverage?
Support enable_clean_error_family_now and adopt_two_step_rollout_sequence. Challenge any attempt to include W0108 in the next tranche.
Agree that W0108 and W0621 can wait, but W0640 should get targeted inspection soon because the closure capture looks correctness-relevant.
Support fix_small_obvious_warning_set_first as scoped; W1514, W1309, and W0107 are small enough for one cleanup pass.
Keep the enable-now warning subset exactly to the probed clean list; do not expand by analogy.
The pragmatic split is coherent: enable-now clean checks, fix-first tiny warning set, defer noisy/policy-heavy items, and review W0640 separately.

next_topic_proposals.text: Should W0640 be treated as a one-off correctness fix to patch immediately in commands/map/links.py without enabling the broader rule family yet?
next_topic_proposals.why: The summary identifies a specific closure-capture case that may have real behavioral impact and needs a targeted decision separate from noisy warning rollout.

next_topic_proposals.text: What exact list of clean-pass E message IDs should be appended to pyproject.toml in the next tranche so the rollout is explicit and reviewable?
next_topic_proposals.why: The panel converged on enabling the clean error family now, but the commit still needs a concrete reviewed ID list.

next_topic_proposals.text: Should the fix-first tranche be split into one commit for code cleanups and a second commit for enabling W1514,W1309,W0107 to keep CI attribution obvious?
next_topic_proposals.why: This determines the safest rollout mechanics under the existing CI job that runs make pylint.
